_note: Tier-2 parts-interchange manifest. This catalogs the large PART-NUMBER interchange datasets that are NOT loaded in Marion's browser (unlike the Tier-1 profile knowledge in /knowledge). These normalize into one common schema and are destined for Supabase ingestion in Phase 3. The style-bridges link this tier back to the Tier-1 parent-profile model.

common_schema.house_brand: brand the cross maps INTO (e.g. Dichtomatik)
common_schema.house_partno: house part number offered as the equivalent
common_schema.house_style: house style/profile code — joins to the profile layer
common_schema.competitor_brand: brand being crossed FROM (SKF, National, Parker, Harwal...)
common_schema.fit_value: numeric fit score where present (0 = exact)

sources.house_family: Freudenberg (Dichtomatik = Freudenberg 'red' line; Freudenberg 'blue' = premium tier)
sources.notes: PART# crossover, not profile crossover. Joins to Tier-1 at the style/profile level via the style-bridge, and at dimensions (shaft/bore/width). 'no_interchange' rows describe competitor parts Dichtomatik has no equivalent for — kept as gap data.

join_strategy.to_tier1_profiles: house_style (+ competitor_style) -> style-bridge -> Fluidseal parent_profile. Dichtomatik/Freudenberg styles seed the Freudenberg column on parent-profiles.json after human review.
join_strategy.by_dimensions: shaft_in / bore_in / width_in let a competitor part resolve to a Fluidseal profile even when no exact part-number cross exists.

supabase_plan.status: deferred to Phase 3 (Train First, Connect Later)
supabase_plan.ingestion_source: the *.jsonl.gz normalized files are the canonical ingestion artifacts
